title: 'podman system reset' won´'t work if podman state is weird
body:
### Issue Description When trying to setup rootless containers, I ran into the issue where I configured storage paths in `~/.config/containers/storage.conf`, yet podman would refuse to work. User errors aside, when I did set up the configuration correctly, I was unable to reset the state with `podman system reset`; I just kept getting `Error: mkdir /run/containers: permission denied` The `--debug` parameter added no information whatsoever (literally.) Eventually a user on IRC pointed me at `--log-level trace`. Running `podman system reset --log-level trace` gave me the following: `DEBU[0000] Overriding run root "/run/user/1000/containers/storage" with "/run/containers/storage" from database` After manually deleting `.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db` the command worked, and podman started functioning properly. ### Steps to reproduce the issue 1. Configure storage.conf with a directory where you don't have permissions 2. Fruitlessly try to use podman 3. Change the path in storage.conf 4. Try to reset podman with `podman system reset` 5. Notice the system still doesn't work ### Describe the results you received Insufficiently informative error messages ### Describe the results you expected I expected `podman system reset` to function even with a borked state, to "unbork" it. ### podman info output
Open Sesame

```yaml host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.29.0 cgroupControllers: - cpu - io - memory - pids cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v2 conmon: package: /usr/bin/conmon is owned by conmon 1:2.1.7-1 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.1.7, commit: f633919178f6c8ee4fb41b848a056ec33f8d707d' cpuUtilization: idlePercent: 90.75 systemPercent: 2.04 userPercent: 7.21 cpus: 8 distribution: distribution: arch version: unknown eventLogger: journald hostname: potatobee idMappings: gidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1000 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 100000 size: 65536 uidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1000 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 100000 size: 65536 kernel: 6.2.2-zen1-1-zen linkmode: dynamic logDriver: journald memFree: 1582710784 memTotal: 16421769216 networkBackend: netavark ociRuntime: name: crun package: /usr/bin/crun is owned by crun 1.8.1-1 path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 1.8.1 commit: f8a096be060b22ccd3d5f3ebe44108517fbf6c30 rundir: /run/user/1000/crun spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL os: linux remoteSocket: path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID rootless: true seccompEnabled: true seccompProfilePath: /etc/containers/seccomp.json selinuxEnabled: false serviceIsRemote: false slirp4netns: executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns package: /usr/bin/slirp4netns is owned by slirp4netns 1.2.0-1 version: |- slirp4netns version 1.2.0 commit: 656041d45cfca7a4176f6b7eed9e4fe6c11e8383 libslirp: 4.7.0 SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 4 libseccomp: 2.5.4 swapFree: 4292055040 swapTotal: 4294963200 uptime: 1h 18m 29.00s (Approximately 0.04 days) plugins: authorization: null log: - k8s-file - none - passthrough - journald network: - bridge - macvlan volume: - local registries: {} store: configFile: /home/alwyn/.config/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 0 paused: 0 running: 0 stopped: 0 graphDriverName: btrfs graphOptions: {} graphRoot: /home/alwyn/.local/share/containers/storage graphRootAllocated: 404181291008 graphRootUsed: 290112475136 graphStatus: Build Version: Btrfs v6.1.3 Library Version: "102" imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp imageStore: number: 0 runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers/storage transientStore: false volumePath: /home/alwyn/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 4.4.2 Built: 1677255177 BuiltTime: Fri Feb 24 17:12:57 2023 GitCommit: 74afe26887f814d1c39925a1624851ef3590e79c-dirty GoVersion: go1.20.1 Os: linux OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 4.4.2 ```

### Podman in a container No ### Privileged Or Rootless Rootless ### Upstream Latest Release Yes ### Additional environment details ArchLinux, followed [this guide](https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/Podman) for setting up rootless podman. ### Additional information Relevant configuration setting is `runroot` under the primary `[storage]` section of `~/.config/containers/storage.conf`.

comments.body: I think that is the expected error, your database was pointing to the wrong directory, probably you were copying these files from another user.
@giuseppe I started off from a completely clean slate. Nothing was copied from anyone; `~/.local/share/containers` did not exist before starting.

so you get the same error even if  `/home/alwyn/.local/share/containers/storage` is empty?

In that case, could you please attach the log you get from `podman --log-level debug system reset`?
got no reply to my previous question. Closing the issue
